exemplar.title: How does the amount of time given (5-second or 30-second) affect the number of logical thinking and analytical questions answered correctly in an IB DP high school?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the amount of time given (5-second or 30-second) affect the number of logical thinking and analytical questions answered correctly in an IB DP high school?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear statement of aim with explicit explanation of relevance to IB examination settings
Accurate description of the dual-process model and explicit link to the IV manipulation
Precise operationalisation of IV (time limits) and DV (number of correct answers) with clear directional hypotheses
weaknesses: Hypothesis could be more concise by explicitly comparing the two time conditions
Link to IB context could be deepened with more critical commentary on Guo et al. (2017)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands comfortably meet the 5â6 band and one strand meets the full 6. Overall the student demonstrates a very good introduction with only minor lapses, warranting 5/6.

strengths: Research design choice (independent measures) is clearly described and well justified to avoid demand characteristics
Sampling technique (opportunity) and participant characteristics are described and their relevance explained
Controlled variables and materials are detailed and choice is justified to prevent confounds
weaknesses: Inconsistent participant totals listed (6 males vs 20 females vs total of 20)
Pilot study introduced variability in the IV (reading time range)
Lacks detail on validation of question-difficulty calibration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands are explained with rationale, placing the work solidly in the top band; full marks (4/4) are appropriate.

strengths: Appropriate use of descriptive and inferential statistics (MannâWhitney U)
Clear presentation of descriptive statistics in a supporting table
Accurate interpretation of statistical findings linked back to the hypothesis
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies regarding normality claims vs test choice
Graphical presentation errors (means vs medians mismatch, inconsistent numerical formatting)
Omission of effect-size reporting limits depth of analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Statistics are correctly applied with minor errors (band 3â4), graphing contains flaws (band 3â4) and interpretation is strong (band 5â6). The overall performance equates to 4/6.

strengths: Findings are discussed with reference to the dual-process model
Strengths and limitations are clearly stated and directly relevant to the investigation
Modifications are explicitly linked to limitations and are justified
weaknesses: Discussion of findings lacks depth and engagement with wider literature or alternative explanations
Some limitation points are under-developed and end abruptly
Conclusion inconsistently states support for the model despite non-significant results
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While strengths/limitations and modifications meet higher bands, the superficial discussion of findings places this criterion at the lower end of the 3â4 band, meriting 3/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar chart with error bars is provided and clearly contrasts the two conditions, thereby addressing the hypothesis.  However, the axis label shows means while the caption references medians, and numerical labels are inconsistent (comma vs point).  These **presentation errors** place the work in the 3-4 band; a mid-band 3 reflects noticeable imperfections.

The student states the Mann-Whitney outcome, interprets the p-value (fail to reject null), and then **links the statistical conclusion back to the hypothesis and descriptive pattern**.  Interpretation is accurate and grounded in data; only modest analytical depth is missed (e.g., effect size), so 5 marks within the top band are appropriate.

Both IV and DV are **correctly stated and operationalised**: IV â time allowed (5 s vs 30 s); DV â number of correct responses out of the same 10 questions.  Research and null hypotheses are expressed clearly with directional wording.  Because the operationalisation is precise (specific durations; specific scoring rule), the work meets the highest descriptor without evident shortcomings, warranting the full 6 marks in this strand.

Sampling technique (opportunity) is described and then **justified** â English classes are said to be numerous and representative; coin-flip random allocation is mentioned.  Characteristics (age, gender, year level) are provided and their relevance to familiarity with exams is explained.  This explanatory element satisfies the 3-4 band; depth is adequate for the upper value, so 4 marks are awarded.

The dual process model is **accurately described** â characteristics of System 1 and System 2 are outlined with supporting citations.  The student then directly explains how the independent-variable manipulation (5 s vs 30 s) is intended to elicit System 1 or System 2 processing, **establishing a clear conceptual bridge** between theory and investigation.  The description is accurate and the connection explicit, so this merits placement in the 5-6 band; a mid-band score of 5 reflects very good but not flawless depth.

Findings are **described with reference to the dual process model**, noting limited support.  Discussion remains surface-levelâthere is little critical engagement with theory (no comparative literature, mechanism analysis).  This matches the 3-4 descriptor; a lower-band 3 is chosen due to limited depth.

The student clearly states the aim â to investigate whether time pressure affects the number of correct answers â and then **explains why this is relevant** in an IB examination context (limited time may undermine critical-thinking aims).  The link to real-world assessment makes the relevance explicit rather than merely identified, fulfilling the descriptor for the top band.  Minor wording lapses do not detract from a sound, explicit explanation, so the strand fits comfortably in the 5-6 band, meriting 5 marks.

Several strengths and limitations are **clearly stated and explained** (e.g., design avoids demand characteristics; participant variability; question difficulty; subject-group representativeness).  Each point is related to the investigation, satisfying the âexplainedâ criterion.  Coverage is adequate for the upper value of the 3-4 band, so 4 marks are awarded.

Controlled variables (reading time, identical question set, wording revisions) and materials (consent forms, answer papers, timer, question slides) are both **detailed and their choice explained** with reference to preventing confounds and ensuring comprehension.  This meets the expectation for âexplainedâ, hence top band with 4 marks.

For each cited limitation, the student proposes a **specific, justified modification** (switch to repeated measures to reduce individual differences, broaden subject groups to improve representativeness, adjust question set to match ability).  The link between limitation and change is explicit and reasons are given, fulfilling the top-band descriptor.  Minor brevity prevents full 6, so 5 marks are assigned.

The independent-measures design is not only described but **explained with rationale** (avoiding demand characteristics).  The explanation shows understanding of why this design was chosen and its implications, matching the top band for Exploration.  Minor elaboration gaps keep it at 4 rather than perfect.

Descriptive statistics (mean, median, SD, range) and one inferential test (Mann-Whitney U) are presented.  The test is appropriate for two independent groups with ordinal/ non-normal data, but the report contains **minor inconsistencies** (claims of normality, small ranking table errors).  Because appropriate statistics are applied yet with some inaccuracy, the work fits the 3-4 band; the stronger end (4) is granted as calculations appear mostly correct.

annotations.comment: The title clearly communicates the studyâs focus on time pressure and cognition, effectively framing the investigation.
The research question states IV and DV clearly but could be more concise by explicitly comparing the two time conditions.
Ensure the Table of Contents entries align exactly with section headings and page numbers to aid navigation.
The description of System 1 and System 2 is concise and accurate, providing a solid theoretical foundation.
Citation formatting (e.g., Stanovich & West, 2000 vs Kahneman, D. 2011) is inconsistent; standardize reference style.
Drawing on Guo et al. (2017) is appropriate, but the link to your IB context could be deepened with critical commentary on their method.
IV and DV are precisely operationalized: specific time limits and correct answer count strengthen clarity.
The research hypothesis is directional and clear but could mention an expected effect size to bolster precision.
The rationale for the independentâsamples design to avoid demand characteristics is well explained.
Opportunity sampling is described clearly, but potential biases from selecting only English classes should be acknowledged.
The demographics statement cites 20 participants but lists 6 males and 20 females; check participant totals for consistency.
Controlling reading time is well justified to prevent confounds related to reading speed differences.
The pilot studyâs adjustment to reading time (5â7 s) introduces variability in the IV; maintain a single reading time for consistency.
The materials description lists question types but lacks detail on how difficulty was validated; include difficulty calibration.
Ethical steps (consent, anonymity) are well documented, enhancing the studyâs integrity.
Random allocation via coin flip is appropriate; specifying the flipping procedure (e.g., number of flips) would improve transparency.
Include a brief summary of standardised instructions rather than full script to keep main text focused.
No outlier test is described; specify and justify a normality check (e.g., ShapiroâWilk) before choosing analyses.
Table 1 effectively presents mean, median, SD and range, supporting clear descriptive analysis.
The text claims normal distribution yet applies MannâWhitney; reconcile distribution assumptions with test choice.
Figure 2âs caption references medians while the graphic shows means; align labels with actual data plotted.
Axis labels mix commas and decimal points inconsistently; use a single convention for numerical formatting.
MannâWhitney report omits effect size; include $$r$$ or comparable metric to quantify practical significance.
Discussion references the dual-process model, yet lacks engagement with broader literature or alternative explanations.
The limitation paragraph ends abruptly; complete the sentence to ensure all limitations are fully articulated.
The proposed repeatedâmeasures design addresses individual differences but should discuss potential order or practice effects.
Expanding the participant pool beyond English classes is a wellâjustified modification to improve representativeness.
Materialâbased modification lacks specifics on which question types to reduce; clarify the exact changes.
The conclusion states support for the model despite nonâsignificant results; reconcile this inconsistency in interpretation.
References follow APA style inconsistently (missing italics, volume numbers); revise for full APA compliance.
